Politics.headline: Trump 'pretty sure' of Iran deal, but can Pakistan-led efforts end the war?
Politics.detail: Al Jazeera reports on the possibility of a deal to end the US-Iran war, with Pakistan playing a key role in facilitating talks.

Politics.headline: US-Israel war on Iran: What's happening on day 31 of attacks
Politics.detail: Al Jazeera provides updates on the ongoing conflict between the US, Israel, and Iran, marking one month of attacks.

Politics.headline: Vice President JD Vance tops CPAC's straw poll to be US president in 2028
Politics.detail: According to Al Jazeera, JD Vance is leading the polls for potential presidential candidates at the Conservative Political Action Conference.

Politics.headline: Reza Pahlavi pledges to 'make Iran great again' at 2026 CPAC conference
Politics.detail: Al Jazeera reports on Reza Pahlavi's speech at CPAC, promising to restore Iran's greatness.

Politics.headline: Cory Booker says Democrats have 'failed this moment' and calls for new leaders
Politics.detail: The Guardian reports Cory Booker's criticism of the Democratic party and his call for new leadership.

Finance & Economy.headline: Oil tops $116 a barrel as Iran accuses US of preparing invasion
Finance & Economy.detail: Crude prices continue to climb as world faces its biggest energy crisis in decades due to escalating tensions.

Finance & Economy.headline: Global shares decline as oil prices soar amid fears about escalation in Iran
Finance & Economy.detail: Asian shares are mostly declining, as worries continue about soaring oil prices and the potential for further escalation in the U.S. war with Iran, impacting global markets.

Finance & Economy.headline: As US Dollar Index Breaks the 100 Mark, the Global Economy Faces a New Stress Test
Finance & Economy.detail: The US dollar has started to strengthen again, driven by the energy shock and geopolitical tensions, testing the key 100 level which is seen as an important point for global markets.

Finance & Economy.headline: Tokyo stocks decline to a 2026 low after yen breaks ¥160 to the dollar
Finance & Economy.detail: Japan's Nikkei 225 stock average sank to a 2026 low as the conflict in the Middle East continued and Brent crude traded above $115 per barrel, increasing speculation about government intervention to strengthen the yen.

Finance & Economy.headline: HKMA Half-Yearly Monetary and Financial Stability Report (March 2026 Issue)
Finance & Economy.detail: The Hong Kong Monetary Authority published its Half-Yearly Monetary and Financial Stability Report, providing detailed analyses of the global and local economy, as well as the monetary and financial conditions in Hong Kong.

Technology.headline: Kyndryl awarded Texas DIR contract for technology services
Technology.detail: Kyndryl has been awarded a contract by the Texas Department of Information Resources (DIR) to provide deliverables-based IT services (DBITS) to state and local government agencies and other eligible public sector organizations.

Technology.headline: Commission adopts €1.5 billion work programme to boost European and Ukrainian defence industry
Technology.detail: The European Commission approved a €1.5 billion work programme under the European Defence Industry Programme (EDIP) to enhance and modernise Europe's defence industry, boost production capacity and secure technological advancement and resilience.

Technology.headline: Gartner Predicts By 2028, Explainable AI Will Drive LLM Observability Investments to 50% for Secure GenAI Deployment
Technology.detail: Gartner forecasts that by 2028, the growing importance of explainable AI (XAI) will drive large language model (LLM) observability investments to 50% of GenAI deployments, up from 15% today.

Technology.headline: Vishal Dalal steps down as CEO of Pismo
Technology.detail: Vishal Dalal has announced his decision to step down as CEO of Brazilian core banking platform Pismo after a year in the role.

Technology.headline: From page to progress: Burgeoning sci-fi reflects China's tech-savvy modernization
Technology.detail: The development of contemporary sci-fi is closely linked to China's modernization, making China a country brimming with a sense of the future.

Science.headline: Scientists Stretched a Liquid and It Snapped Like a Solid
Science.detail: Scientists have discovered that under the right conditions, ordinary liquids can snap apart like solid objects when stretched with enough force.

Science.headline: Artemis 2 arrives at launch pad for moon launch
Science.detail: NASA's Artemis 2 moon rocket has arrived at its new launch pad in Florida, preparing for its upcoming launch, which will send astronauts around the moon. The Space Launch System rocket reached Launch Complex 36B after an 11 1/2 hour journey from its assembly building.

Science.headline: Manchester scientists discover one of the most common genetic causes of severe epilepsy, offering new hope for thousands of families worldwide
Science.detail: Scientists in Manchester have made a breakthrough in identifying a new condition, "Recessive RNU2-2-related neurodevelopmental disorder", which is one of the most common genetic causes of severe epilepsy in children, providing answers and potential treatments for affected families. The research, published in Nature Genetics, has identified 84 individuals with the condition and estimates that thousands more remain undiagnosed.

Science.headline: Study identifies key neurons that can jump-start leg movement after spinal cord injury
Science.detail: A new study has identified specific neurons that, when transplanted into injured spinal cords, can help restore leg movement. Researchers transplanted neural progenitor cells into animal models and found that activating a small subset of these transplanted neurons led to leg muscle response, indicating the grafted cells had integrated into the spinal cord's motor circuitry.

Science.headline: Royal Society announces 27 new Places of Science grants to small museums
Science.detail: The Royal Society has awarded 27 grants to small museums across the UK to engage communities with local science stories, supporting projects that explore various themes from historical crafts to local scientific figures. The grants will help museums reach new audiences and bring the history of science to life in creative ways, highlighting the role of science in daily life.

Health.headline: Pandemic Agreement Talks Get Five More Negotiating Days
Health.detail: World Health Organization (WHO) member states curtailed talks on the Pandemic Agreement annex and will meet again within a month for further negotiations to ensure rapid sharing of pathogens and fair distribution of benefits like vaccines.

Health.headline: Gary Woodland wins Houston Open after brain surgery and PTSD struggles: 'Today was a good day'
Health.detail: Gary Woodland wins Houston Open after brain surgery and PTSD struggles.

Health.headline: Allergan Aesthetics Releases Findings on the Safety and Efficacy of Repeat TrenibotE Treatments and Data on Medical Weight Loss Aesthetics Patient Behaviors at the 2026 American Academy of Dermatology Meeting (AAD)
Health.detail: Allergan Aesthetics presented data at the American Academy of Dermatology Meeting (AAD) highlighting the safety and efficacy of TrenibotE, an investigational botulinum neurotoxin, and insights into aesthetic concerns of patients undergoing medical weight loss treatments.

Health.headline: US: Rights-Respecting Approaches to Mental Health Crisis
Health.detail: Human Rights Watch and other organizations released a report highlighting alternative models for mental health crisis response that prioritize non-police interventions, consent, and peer involvement, noting that police involvement often escalates crises.

Health.headline: Inside The High-Stakes Corporate Fight Over Feeding Preterm Babies
Health.detail: KFF Health News reports on a high-stakes corporate fight between Abbott Laboratories and Mead Johnson Nutrition over nutritional supplements for preterm infants in neonatal intensive care units, including concerns about potential complications.

Sports.headline: Stefan Bergh Takes Up Role as ITTF Secretary General
Sports.detail: Stefan Bergh has been appointed as the Secretary General of the International Table Tennis Federation, bringing extensive experience in international sport governance to the position as the sport approaches its centenary. Bergh previously served as Secretary General and CEO of the Swedish Sports Confederation since 2016.

Sports.headline: Back-to-back baseball champions
Sports.detail: The ADF Masters (Over 35s) baseball team secured back-to-back championships at the Australian Federal Police Baseball Championships, remaining undefeated across two days of play. The team demonstrated professionalism and teamwork, exemplifying core ADF values on and off the field, ultimately winning the grand final 7-4 against a composite All Star team.

Sports.headline: MLB Athletics 2 Toronto 5
Sports.detail: The Toronto Blue Jays defeated the Oakland Athletics with a score of 5-2 in MLB action on Monday, March 30, 2026. Other MLB scores from the day include Kansas City 4, Atlanta 1 and Minnesota 6, Baltimore 8.

Sports.headline: Ball-tampering in PSL 2026: Haris Rauf, Shaheen Afridi, Fakhar Zaman under fire after viral clip
Sports.detail: Haris Rauf, Shaheen Afridi and Fakhar Zaman are facing scrutiny due to alleged ball-tampering during PSL 2026, following a viral clip. The incident has sparked controversy and raised questions about the integrity of the game.

Sports.headline: 'The problem is...': Dogs' sorry 'deja vu' as Ciraldo's spine switch highlights unsolved problem
Sports.detail: The Canterbury Bulldogs are struggling to score points in the NRL, scoring only 45 points in three games, which is a recurring issue. Coach Cameron Ciraldo made a spine shift during their loss to the Knights, with Stephen Crichton joining the halves and Matt Burton moving to the centres.

Entertainment.headline: Junos 2026: Joni Mitchell, Rush make Junos history, the Beaches take home record-breaking win
Entertainment.detail: The 2026 Juno Awards in Canada honored both emerging artists and established icons like Joni Mitchell and Rush, marking a historic night for Canadian music. The Beaches also achieved a record-breaking win.

Entertainment.headline: Telematrix No. 115: March 30, 2026 Can you name a Sarah Michelle Gellar or David Boreanaz TV show with 3+ seasons?
Entertainment.detail: Vulture poses a trivia question related to Sarah Michelle Gellar and David Boreanaz TV shows with at least 3 seasons, engaging readers with entertainment-related puzzles.

Entertainment.headline: 'Dhurandhar 2' BOday 12 [LIVE]: Ranveer Singh film eyes Rs 1000 crore net
Entertainment.detail: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' continues its box office success, aiming for Rs 1000 crore net in India after surpassing its predecessor's lifetime earnings in just 11 days. The film has already grossed over Rs 1,361.95 crore globally.

Entertainment.headline: Tiger Woods' Mugshot 2026: See the Photo of the Golfer After His DUI Arrest
Entertainment.detail: Following his arrest and charge for suspicion of DUI, Tiger Woods' mugshot has been released to the public. This comes after Woods was released from jail after a DUI arrest.

Entertainment.headline: Peter Alexander Leaves NBC News After 22 Years
Entertainment.detail: Peter Alexander is leaving NBC News after 22 years, after balancing his time as the White House correspondent and co-anchor on the Today show.

Crime & Justice.headline: Central Haitian town descends into fire and bloodshed from gang warfare
Crime & Justice.detail: A central Haitian town has been engulfed in flames and bloodshed due to intense gang conflict, causing widespread devastation and displacement.

Crime & Justice.headline: France probes possible Iran link after bomb attack foiled outside Bank of America
Crime & Justice.detail: French authorities are investigating a potential connection to Iran following the disruption of a bomb attack that targeted a Bank of America building in Paris.

Crime & Justice.headline: Deaths of 3-year-old child, 3 adults in Queens fire deemed homicides.
Crime & Justice.detail: A fire in Queens that resulted in the deaths of a 3-year-old child and three adults has been ruled as a homicide.

Crime & Justice.headline: Parents prosecuted after baby tests positive for meth, brother's teeth rotten almost to the gums
Crime & Justice.detail: In New Zealand, parents are facing prosecution after their baby tested positive for methamphetamine, and the baby's brother suffered from severe tooth decay.

Crime & Justice.headline: Executed killer's chilling claim about what she felt during pickax murders still haunts retired FBI agent
Crime & Justice.detail: A retired FBI agent remains haunted by the chilling claim of an executed killer regarding her feelings during pickax murders.

Education.headline: National safety education day for middle and primary school students marked across China
Education.detail: China marked its national safety education day for middle and primary school students on March 30, 2026, with schools organizing various safety drills and exercises. Activities included fire extinguisher training, traffic safety gesture exercises, and school bus emergency escape drills.

Education.headline: NC Symphony kicks off its multi-year residency at Wilson County Schools with a concert
Education.detail: The North Carolina Symphony commenced a multi-year residency at Wilson County Schools with a concert on March 30, 2026. Joe Peters, associate principal of the oboe and English horn, highlighted the diverse composers featured, including those from North Carolina.

Education.headline: Rehabilitation at risk as prison education provision shrinks
Education.detail: A report highlights a decline in education within Jersey's prison, HMP La Moye, due to staffing cuts. Education staffing has decreased from five teachers in 2019 to 2.84 full-time equivalents in 2025, despite a rising prison population, leading to paused classes and demotivated inmates.

Education.headline: Colorado bill would make sure charter schools are considered in local construction ballot measures
Education.detail: Senate Bill 145 in Colorado aims to ensure charter schools receive consideration in local construction ballot measures. The bill would require districts to solicit and consider charter school construction proposals and establish new timelines for collaboration on ballot initiatives.

Education.headline: School Assembly News Headlines Today – March 30, 2026
Education.detail: Schools across India are actively preparing students for board examinations and competitive entrance tests through revision classes and counseling. Educational institutions are adopting modern teaching methods focused on skill development and critical thinking, incorporating smart classrooms and online learning platforms.

Lifestyle.headline: Cruise passengers complained about cabin problem, now ships are making a big change
Lifestyle.detail: Cruise lines are responding to passenger complaints about cabin issues by implementing significant changes to improve the onboard experience.

Lifestyle.headline: Ben Carson points to Gen Z church revival as young Americans push back on secular culture
Lifestyle.detail: Ben Carson highlights a resurgence of faith among Gen Z, suggesting a rejection of secular values and a return to religious institutions.

Lifestyle.headline: Worries about flying seem to be taking off. Here's how to cope with in-flight anxiety.
Lifestyle.detail: With anxieties surrounding air travel on the rise, experts offer advice and strategies for managing and alleviating in-flight anxiety.

Lifestyle.headline: Electric bikes can be fast and dangerous. Here's how to stay safe.
Lifestyle.detail: As electric bikes become more popular, safety concerns are growing; this article provides essential tips for safely operating e-bikes.

Lifestyle.headline: Duchess Sophie's fashion icon moment in Princess Diana-coded 'revenge' dress
Lifestyle.detail: Duchess Sophie made a style statement reminiscent of Princess Diana with her choice of dress, sparking comparisons and admiration.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Certain hardship in uncertain times: the economic ramifications of the US/Israeli strikes on Iran
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The ongoing war between the United States, Israel, and Iran has led to deteriorating prospects for the global economy, with potential economic damage outlasting the military campaign itself; Iran has effectively blockaded the Strait of Hormuz, causing sharp increases in energy prices. Sectors like tourism and artificial intelligence in the Gulf are undermined by the conflict.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Outdated court orders are forcing Alabama schools to discriminate by race
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Despite Alabama schools having long desegregated, outdated court orders from the 1960s still mandate racial discrimination. These orders continue to influence school policies despite the progress made in desegregation.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial: We Are The Ones We've Been Waiting For
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Ordinary people are taking actions based on empathy and genuine concern for their communities, reflecting a resurgence of decency. These actions address community needs and demonstrate a commitment to the country's core values.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: [ED] De-escalation needed in Hormuz
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Korea Times emphasizes that de-escalation of tensions in the Strait of Hormuz is a shared responsibility, requiring Iran to halt destabilizing actions and the United States to prioritize diplomatic engagement. Prolonged disruption to energy supplies would have global repercussions.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: From lobster to laptops, organized crime is targeting U.S. freight
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Organized criminal networks are increasingly targeting the U.S. trucking industry, employing sophisticated methods to steal cargo, impersonate legitimate companies, and operate across state lines. These thefts threaten the reliability of the supply chain and require Congressional action to strengthen the fight against these criminal syndicates.

World.headline: Trump Mulls Seizing Iranian Island Even as Diplomatic Talks Appear to Be Moving Ahead
World.detail: Amid ongoing tensions, former President Trump is reportedly considering seizing an Iranian island, even as diplomatic talks show signs of progress, raising concerns about escalating conflict in the region.

World.headline: Over 70 Killed in South Sudan in Dispute Over Gold Mine
World.detail: A violent dispute over a gold mine in South Sudan has resulted in the deaths of over 70 people, highlighting the challenges of resource management and conflict resolution in the country.

World.headline: Oil tops $116 a barrel as Iran accuses US of preparing invasion
World.detail: With escalating tensions in the Middle East, particularly surrounding Iran, the price of oil has surged to over $116 a barrel as Iran accuses the US of planning a ground invasion.

World.headline: UN peacekeeper killed in southern Lebanon as Israeli invasion intensifies.
World.detail: As the Israeli invasion of southern Lebanon intensifies, a UN peacekeeper has been killed, raising concerns about the safety of international personnel and the escalating conflict in the region.

World.headline: Spain closes airspace to US warplanes in further rebuke to Trump
World.detail: Amid rising international tensions, Spain has closed its airspace to US warplanes in a further rebuke to President Trump's foreign policy decisions.

Zimbabwe.headline: BREAKING: Marinica punishes Teenage
Zimbabwe.detail: The Zimbabwe Football Association has confirmed Teenage Hadebe's suspension from the Mukuru 4 Nations final against Zambia.

Zimbabwe.headline: Chiwenga clashes with Mnangagwa loyalists over constitutional amendment
Zimbabwe.detail: Civil society leaders have alleged selective policing, with authorities accused of blocking meetings organised by anti-amendment groups while permitting pro-government gatherings to proceed unhindered, leading to rising tensions.

Zimbabwe.headline: CAF Introduces New Rules to Restore Trust After AFCON 2025 Controversies
Zimbabwe.detail: CAF is implementing new regulations aimed at rebuilding trust following controversies at the AFCON 2025.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zim leads regional ranking on exam fraud
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe is leading regionally in exam fraud.

Zimbabwe.headline: Marinica insists on merit for Warriors selection
Zimbabwe.detail: Warriors coach Marian Marinica is emphasizing merit-based selection for the team.
